title: NOC 20012 – Computer and Information Systems Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Computer and Information Systems Managers lead digital transformation initiatives, oversee enterprise IT infrastructure, and manage software, cloud, and cybersecurity teams across public and private organizations in Canada. Classified under NOC 20012, this TEER 0 management occupation continues to experience strong nationwide demand—creating premium immigration opportunities through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.major_group: 200 – Specialized middle management occupations in engineering, architecture, science, and information systems
classification.minor_group: 2001 – Managers in engineering, architecture, science, and information systems

common_job_titles: Data processing and systems analysis manager
duties: Computer and Information Systems Managers may perform some or all of the following:
Direct IT and electronic data-processing departments
Establish technical policies and system-development procedures
Consult with clients on system requirements and budgets
Lead software, network, and infrastructure teams
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, engineering, business, or commerce
Extensive experience in systems analysis, software engineering, or network design
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 42,600 new positions for Computer and Information Systems Managers, while around 53,400 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and executive-level immigration planning especially important for overseas professionals.
additional_information: Computer and Information Systems Managers remain in high demand across Canada’s technology-driven economy, with strong recruitment in cloud computing, cybersecurity, fintech, healthcare IT, and government digital services.

Technology companies and IT consultanciesGovernment departments and agenciesFinancial institutions and fintech firmsHealthcare networksTelecommunications providersManufacturing and logistics enterprises
Nationwide digital-transformation programsCloud and AI adoptionCybersecurity expansionPublic-sector IT modernizationSenior-level technical leadership needs
Annual salaries typically range between $67,100 and $148,000 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and executive experience.
